name: future_value
description: Calculate the future value of a single lump-sum investment with compound interest. Use this when you know the initial principal, annual interest rate, and time horizon and need the final accumulated amount. Do not use this for comparing multiple rate/period combinations—use future_value_table instead. Returns the compounded value after the specified years, plus total interest earned.

principal.description: Initial investment amount in currency units (e.g., 10000 for $10,000). Must be a positive number.

rate.description: Annual interest rate as a decimal (e.g., 0.05 for 5%, not 5). Must be non-negative.

years.description: Number of years until the investment matures. Must be a positive integer.

compound_frequency.description: Number of times per year that interest compounds. Valid values: 1 (annual), 4 (quarterly), 12 (monthly), 365 (daily). Defaults to 1 if omitted.

name: present_value
description: Calculate the present value (current worth) of a known future amount discounted at a given annual rate. Use this for loan valuation, bond pricing, or determining how much a future payment is worth today. Do not use this for comparing multiple discount rates—use future_value_table instead, passing different rates to see how present value changes.

future_value.description: Future amount to be discounted back to present value in currency units (e.g., 20000 for $20,000). Must be a positive number.

rate.description: Annual discount rate as a decimal (e.g., 0.05 for 5%, not 5). Must be non-negative.

years.description: Number of years until the future amount is received. Must be a positive integer.

compound_frequency.description: Number of times per year that discount compounds. Valid values: 1 (annual), 4 (quarterly), 12 (monthly), 365 (daily). Defaults to 1 if omitted.

name: discount
description: Calculate the final price and savings amount after applying a single percentage discount to an original price. Use this for retail pricing, sale calculations, or determining sale prices from a known discount percentage. Do not use this for comparing multiple discount percentages—use discount_table instead.

price.description: Original price before discount in currency units (e.g., 100 for $100). Must be a non-negative number.

discount.description: Discount percentage as a whole number (e.g., 20 for 20% discount, not 0.20). Accepts values from 0 to 100. Values greater than 1 are treated as percentages; values ≤1 are treated as decimals.

name: markup
description: Calculate the selling price, markup amount, and profit margin from a cost price and markup percentage. Use this for product pricing, setting retail prices, or determining profit margins. Do not use this for comparing multiple markup percentages—use individual calls with different markup values instead (no markup_table tool exists).

cost.description: Cost price of the product in currency units (e.g., 100 for $100). Must be a non-negative number.

markup.description: Markup percentage as a whole number (e.g., 30 for 30% markup, not 0.30). Accepts values from 0 to 100. Values greater than 1 are treated as percentages; values ≤1 are treated as decimals.

name: future_value_table
description: Generate a comparison table of future values across multiple interest rates and time periods. Use this for investment scenario comparison, rate sensitivity analysis, and long-term planning where you need to see how different rate/period combinations affect the final value. Each row shows one rate/period combination with its resulting future value. Do not use this for single calculations—use future_value instead.

principal.description: Initial investment amount in currency units (e.g., 10000 for $10,000). Must be a positive number.

rates.description: Interest rates to compare, as decimals (e.g., [0.03, 0.05, 0.07] for 3%, 5%, 7%). Each rate must be a non-negative number. Use whole numbers multiplied by 0.01 for readability.

periods.description: Time periods in years to compare (e.g., [1, 5, 10, 20]). Each period must be a positive integer.

name: discount_table
description: Compare multiple discount percentages for the same original price. Use this for bulk pricing strategies, promotional planning, and price sensitivity analysis. Each row shows one discount percentage with its resulting final price and discount amount. Do not use this for single discount calculations—use discount instead.

price.description: Original price before discount in currency units (e.g., 100 for $100). Must be a non-negative number.

discounts.description: Discount percentages to compare as whole numbers (e.g., [10, 15, 20, 25, 30] for 10%, 15%, 20%, etc.). Each value should be 0-100. Values >1 are treated as percentages.

name: launch_ui
description: Launch an interactive web-based financial calculator with a tabbed interface. Use this when you need a visual interface with real-time calculations, interactive tables, and gradient UI for exploring financial scenarios. Do not use this for programmatic or batch calculations—use the individual calculation tools instead. Returns the port and URL where the UI is accessible.

port.description: Port number for the web server. Must be in the valid range 1024-65535. Defaults to 5050 if omitted.
